q_tail.question: Does the hindwing have a tail?
q_tail.hint: Check the posterior margin of the hindwing for a projection. Most Arhopala tails are at vein 2.

choices.label: Yes — hindwing is tailed
No — hindwing is tailless

q_tailed_vein3.question: Is the tail short, white-tipped, and located at vein 3 (one vein forward from the usual vein 2)?
q_tailed_vein3.hint: The abseus group has a distinctively short, white-tipped tail at vein 3, not the usual vein 2. Also check for a spot at the base of hindwing space 1a.

choices.label: Yes — white-tipped tail at vein 3, spot at base of HW space 1a
No — tail at the usual position (vein 2)

q_tailed_long_tail.question: Is the tail notably long and filamentous — roughly 3.5 mm or more?
q_tailed_long_tail.hint: Two groups have unusually long tails: the corinda group (~3.5 mm) and the aedias group (~6.0 mm). Both are distinctly longer than typical Arhopala tails. If uncertain, answer 'No' and proceed.

choices.label: Yes — tail is notably long (roughly 3.5–6 mm or more)
No — tail is short and typical

q_tailed_long_dark.question: On the forewing underside, is the basal half of space 1b completely darkened?
q_tailed_long_dark.hint: This is the key difference between the two long-tailed groups. The corinda group has the basal half of FW underside space 1b completely darkened. The aedias group has a long tail (~6.0 mm) but lacks this darkening — the FW underside is normally marked.

choices.label: Yes — basal half of FW underside space 1b completely darkened (~3.5 mm tail)
No — FW underside normally marked, no darkening (~6.0 mm tail)

q_tailed_ganesa.question: Is this a small species with spots above the forewing cell and a spot at the base of forewing space 3?
q_tailed_ganesa.hint: The ganesa group is characteristically small, with spots visible above the FW cell on the underside, plus a spot at the base of FW space 3. All ganesa have short tails.

choices.label: Yes — small; spots above FW cell; spot at base of FW space 3
No

q_tailed_cell_silver.question: Are the cell spots on the forewing underside outlined in greenish silver?
q_tailed_cell_silver.hint: Examine the underside forewing cell. The centaurus group has a distinctive metallic greenish-silver outline around the cell spots — this is not iridescence but a true outline.

choices.label: Yes — greenish silver outlines on FW cell spots
No — no silver outlines

q_tailed_male_green.question: Is the male upperside shining green?
q_tailed_male_green.hint: Most Arhopala males are shining blue or purple. The eumolphus and aurea groups are among the few with shining green males. Answer 'No' for females or blue/purple males.

choices.label: Yes — shining green upperside (male)
No — blue, purple, brown, or female

q_tailed_green_type.question: How does the forewing postdiscal spot 4 relate to spots 5 and 6, and what is the hindwing cell length?
q_tailed_green_type.hint: Eumolphus: FW spot 4 is moved out of line with the others; HW spot 6 is placed directly below spot 7. Aurea: HW cell is considerably longer than half the wing length; FW apex is acute with a straight or concave termen.

choices.label: FW spot 4 out of line; HW spot 6 directly below spot 7
HW cell considerably longer than half wing; FW apex acute with straight or concave termen

q_tailed_dark_area.question: Is there a distinct dark area under the forewing cell (space 1b), bounded outwardly by a pale line?
q_tailed_dark_area.hint: The camdeo group has a characteristic darkened patch in FW underside space 1b with a clearly defined pale line running across it. This feature is diagnostic for the entire camdeo group.

choices.label: Yes — distinct dark patch in FW space 1b with pale bordering line
No — no such marking

q_camdeo_sub.question: Within the camdeo group, what additional features are present?
q_camdeo_sub.hint: The three camdeo subgroups each have a distinct secondary feature on top of the dark FW space 1b patch.

choices.label: Crimson-brown underside with markings outlined in white striae; spot at base of HW space 6
Spot present mid-space 8 on the hindwing underside
Small spot at the extreme base of forewing space 3
None of the above — camdeo features only

q_tailed_ijanensis.question: In the male, does the forewing upperside black border run along the costa all the way to the wing base?
q_tailed_ijanensis.hint: This is unique to the ijanensis group: the male's FW costa border extends to the base. Also check for a spot at the base of hindwing space 6.

choices.label: Yes — male FW border extends to base of costa; spot at base of HW space 6
No

q_tailed_anthelus.question: Is the forewing postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 4, with at least 3 spots in FW space 11 AND a subbasal spot in HW space 8?
q_tailed_anthelus.hint: The anthelus group has a distinctive combination: partial dislocation of the FW postdiscal band at vein 4, multiple (≥3) spots in FW space 11, and a subbasal spot in HW space 8.

choices.label: Yes — FW band partially dislocated at vein 4; ≥3 spots in FW space 11; subbasal spot in HW space 8
No

q_tailed_macular.question: Are the underside markings macular — composed of separated lines and dots rather than continuous solid bands?
q_tailed_macular.hint: The democritus group has markings that are broken up into isolated dots and short lines, not forming a continuous postdiscal band. The HW postdiscal band is also widely dislocated at vein 2.

choices.label: Yes — macular markings (dots/lines); HW postdiscal band widely dislocated at vein 2
No — markings form more continuous bands

q_tailed_alitaeus.question: Is there a central spot in forewing underside space 11?
q_tailed_alitaeus.hint: The alitaeus group is defined by having a central spot in FW space 11 (above the cell). Note: the anthelus group has ≥3 spots in space 11 but was already checked. The alitaeus group also has a distinctive right-angled male aedeagus (requires dissection).

choices.label: Yes — central spot in FW space 11
No

q_tailed_cleander.question: Is this a relatively large species with a conspicuously projecting tornal lobe on the hindwing, and postdiscal spots 6 and 7 placed vertically?
q_tailed_cleander.hint: The cleander group is distinguished by its large size and the prominent projecting tornal lobe (bulge at the anal angle of the hindwing). The postdiscal band is dislocated at vein 2, with spots 6 and 7 stacked vertically.

choices.label: Yes — large; conspicuous projecting HW tornal lobe; spots 6 and 7 vertical
No — or tornal lobe less conspicuous

q_cleander_sub.question: How prominent is the tornal lobe?
q_cleander_sub.hint: The alea subgroup consists of smaller species within the cleander group, with a less conspicuous tornal lobe.

choices.label: Smaller species; tornal lobe less conspicuous
Larger species; tornal lobe conspicuously projecting

q_tailed_agrata.question: What is the shape of the hindwing underside central cell spot, and are there specific absences on the forewing underside?
q_tailed_agrata.hint: The agrata group has two subgroups: ace (with a band-like cell spot across the whole HW cell) and agrata (lacking a FW postdiscal spot in space 9 and lacking a costal spot in space 10).

choices.label: HW central cell spot is band-like, stretching entirely across the cell (ace subgroup)
FW lacks postdiscal spot in space 9; no costal spot in FW space 10 (agrata subgroup)
Neither of these

q_tailed_spot_arrangement.question: What best describes the forewing postdiscal spot 4 and the hindwing postdiscal spot 6?
q_tailed_spot_arrangement.hint: This question separates the remaining tailed groups by the arrangement of key spots. Examine spot 4 on the FW postdiscal band and the position of spot 6 on the HW underside.

choices.label: FW spot 4 out of line with others; HW spot 6 placed directly below spot 7
FW spot 4 in continuation with spots 5 and 6; HW spot 6 rounded and more or less separated
HW spot 6 is outwardly sinuous; costal spot present in FW space 10
FW postdiscal band is continuous and unbroken; no costal spot in FW space 10; often a spot at base of FW space 3
HW postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 overlap with their centres forming a straight line (echelon)

q_amphimuta_sub.question: Within the amphimuta group (echelon HW spots 5–7), which subgroup best fits the specimen?
q_amphimuta_sub.hint: Four subgroups. Muta: spots more quadrate/banded, FW space 2 stepped toward termen. Perimuta: FW spots 2 and 3 in line. Kurzi: tailless, rounded HW tornus, HW band widely dislocated at vein 2. Amphimuta (largest, 12 spp.): spots rounded, cilia often prolonged into a short tooth at vein 2.

choices.label: Spots more quadrate/banded; FW space 2 spot stepped outward toward termen (muta subgroup)
FW postdiscal spots in spaces 2 and 3 are in line (perimuta subgroup)
Tailless; rounded HW tornus; HW postdiscal band widely dislocated at vein 2 (kurzi subgroup)
Spots rounded; cilia often prolonged into a short tooth at vein 2 (amphimuta subgroup)

q_tailless_fulla.question: Does the forewing underside completely lack an end-cell bar, and are the markings generally obscure or faint?
q_tailless_fulla.hint: The fulla group is unusual in lacking the end-cell bar on the FW underside. The overall underside markings are usually indistinct or obscure.

choices.label: Yes — no end-cell bar on FW underside; markings obscure
No — end-cell bar present or markings clear

q_tailless_inornata.question: Does the hindwing have a very rounded tornus, and are tornal green scales absent?
q_tailless_inornata.hint: The inornata group has a distinctively rounded hindwing tornus (anal angle). Unlike many other tailless Arhopala, it lacks metallic green or brassy tornal scales.

choices.label: Yes — very rounded HW tornus; no tornal green scales
No

q_tailless_hypomuta.question: Is there a white spot in the cilia of hindwing space 1a?
q_tailless_hypomuta.hint: The hypomuta group has a distinctive white spot in the cilia (fringe) of HW space 1a. Also usually has a spot at the extreme base of HW space 6 on the underside.

choices.label: Yes — white spot in HW space 1a cilia
No

q_tailless_oenea.question: Is the forewing apex acute with a straight termen, and do the hindwing underside postdiscal spots form narrow, regular, rectangular shapes?
q_tailless_oenea.hint: The oenea group is characterized by a pointed FW apex with a straight termen edge, and distinctively narrow, rectangular (not rounded) postdiscal spots on the HW underside.

choices.label: Yes — FW apex acute and termen straight; HW postdiscal spots narrow and rectangular
No

q_tailless_agesias.question: Are the underside spots prominent and rounded, with a central spot in FW space 11 and a notably short forewing vein 9?
q_tailless_agesias.hint: The agesias group has large, rounded, prominent spots (not narrow bands). Key features: central spot in FW space 11 (above the cell), and FW vein 9 is noticeably shorter than usual.

choices.label: Yes — prominent rounded spots; central FW spot in space 11; FW vein 9 notably short
No

q_tailless_epimuta.question: Does the male have a distinctly differently-coloured or more opaque discal patch on the forewing upperside?
q_tailless_epimuta.hint: In the epimuta group, males have a discal patch on the FW upperside that is noticeably more opaque or differently textured than the surrounding shining blue. On the underside, HW postdiscal spot 6 is placed midway between spot 5 and the end-cell bar.

choices.label: Yes — male FW upperside has opaque or differently-coloured discal patch
No

q_tailless_agesilaus.question: On the hindwing underside, is postdiscal spot 6 placed equidistantly between spot 5 and the end-cell bar?
q_tailless_agesilaus.hint: The agesilaus group can be confused with the amphimuta group. Its defining feature is HW spot 6 placed equidistant from spot 5 and the end-cell bar — not closer to one or the other. Tailless.

choices.label: Yes — HW spot 6 equidistant between spot 5 and end-cell bar
No

q_tailless_agelastus_check.question: Is the forewing underside postdiscal band continuous and unbroken, often with a small spot at the base of space 3?
q_tailless_agelastus_check.hint: The agelastus group has a continuous (non-dislocated) FW postdiscal band, often with a spot at the base of FW space 3. Most species lack a costal spot in FW space 10, though one species (epimete suedas) is an exception.

choices.label: Yes — FW postdiscal band continuous; often a spot at base of FW space 3
No

g_centaurus.description: Separated from all other groups by having the cell spots on the forewing underside outlined in greenish silver. The hindwing cell is short — significantly less than half the wing length.
key_features: FW cell spots outlined in greenish silver (underside)
HW cell short: noticeably less than half the wing length
Tailed

g_anthelus.description: Characterised by the forewing postdiscal band being only partially dislocated at vein 4. Extra spots are prominent: at least three in FW space 11 and a subbasal spot in HW space 8.
key_features: FW postdiscal band partially dislocated at vein 4
At least 3 spots in FW space 11
Subbasal spot in HW space 8

g_camdeo_general.description: Defined by a dark area under the forewing cell bounded outwardly by a pale line across space 1b. HW postdiscal spots 7, 6, and 5 are arranged in echelon. No additional subgroup features detected.
key_features: Dark area under FW cell (space 1b) bounded by pale line
HW postdiscal spots 7, 6, 5 in echelon

g_camdeo_camdeo.description: The core camdeo subgroup within the camdeo group. Often features a small spot at the extreme base of space 3 on the forewing underside, in addition to the dark space 1b area.
key_features: Dark area under FW cell (space 1b) bounded by pale line
Small spot at extreme base of FW space 3
HW postdiscal spots 7, 6, 5 in echelon

g_camdeo_myrzala.description: Recognizable by a crimson-brown underside with markings outlined by white striae. Always has a spot at the base of hindwing space 6.
key_features: Crimson-brown underside
Markings outlined by white striae
Spot at base of HW space 6
Dark area under FW cell (space 1b)

g_camdeo_bazaloides.description: Unique within the camdeo group for having a spot positioned mid-space 8 on the hindwing underside.
key_features: Spot mid-space 8 on HW underside
Dark area under FW cell (space 1b)

g_agesias.description: Tailless, with slightly dentate (toothed) HW margins. Both sexes similar: shining, powdery blue with broad black borders (~1.5 mm at tornus, expanding to mid-costa). Prominent rounded spots on the underside; at least one subcostal spot in FW space 11. FW vein 9 notably short. Three Malaysian species.
key_features: Tailless (HW margins slightly dentate)
Sexes alike: shining powdery blue; broad black borders
At least one subcostal spot above FW cell in space 11
FW vein 9 notably short

g_ijanensis.description: Tailed species where the male upperside forewing border uniquely runs along the costa all the way to the base of the wing. A spot is present at the base of hindwing space 6.
key_features: Tailed
Male: FW costa border runs to wing base (unique to this group)
Spot at base of HW space 6

g_epimuta.description: Males are instantly recognizable by a discal patch on the forewing upperside that is differently coloured and more opaque than the surrounding blue. On the underside, HW postdiscal spot 6 is placed midway between spot 5 and the end-cell bar.
key_features: Male: opaque or differently-coloured discal patch on FW upperside
HW postdiscal spot 6 midway between spot 5 and end-cell bar
Tailless

g_agesilaus.description: Tailless species that can be confused with the amphimuta group. Defined by HW postdiscal spot 6 placed equidistantly between spot 5 and the end-cell bar. HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Two Malaysian species.
key_features: Tailless
HW postdiscal spot 6 equidistant between spot 5 and end-cell bar
HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2

q_agesilaus_s1.question: Is a basal cell spot present on the forewing underside?
q_agesilaus_s1.hint: Arhopala agesilaus gesa has a basal cell spot on the FW underside (typical for the genus). Arhopala avatha uniquely lacks this basal cell spot — its absence is diagnostic.

choices.label: Yes — basal cell spot present on FW underside
No — basal cell spot absent (unique within this group)

r_agesilaus_gesa.name: Arhopala agesilaus gesa
r_agesilaus_gesa.note: FW underside has a basal cell spot. Male: rich violet blue; FW black border 2.0 mm at tornus expanding to 4.0 mm at apex. Female: bluer shade than male. Superficially resembles a small Arhopala major.

r_avatha.name: Arhopala avatha
r_avatha.note: Uniquely lacks the basal cell spot on FW underside. Male: very dark bluish violet; FW black border 1.0 mm at tornus increasing to 2.0 mm at apex (narrow borders). Female: purple. Similar to Arhopala antimuta but distinguished by wider borders in the male.

g_oenea.description: Forewing apex is acute and the termen is straight. The hindwing underside postdiscal spots are narrow, regular, and rectangular.
key_features: FW apex acute; termen straight
HW postdiscal spots narrow, regular, rectangular
Tailless

g_amphimuta_muta.description: Postdiscal spots are more quadrate (squarish/banded). The FW postdiscal spot in space 2 is stepped outward toward the termen, offset from the main band. Three Malaysian species.
key_features: HW spots 5, 6, 7 in echelon (centres in straight line)
Spots more quadrate/banded in shape
FW space 2 spot stepped outward toward termen

g_amphimuta_perimuta.description: The FW postdiscal spots in spaces 2 and 3 are in line with each other. Two Malaysian species, one easily recognized by a yellowish discal area on the HW underside.
key_features: HW spots 5, 6, 7 in echelon (centres in straight line)
FW postdiscal spots in spaces 2 and 3 are in line

g_hypomuta.description: Tailless species with a white spot in the cilia of hindwing space 1a. Usually possesses a spot at the extreme base of HW space 6 on the underside.
key_features: White spot in HW space 1a cilia
Usually: spot at extreme base of HW space 6
Tailless

g_inornata.description: Tailless with a very rounded hindwing tornus. The underside lacks tornal green scales.
key_features: Very rounded HW tornus
No tornal green scales on underside
Tailless

g_democritus.description: Underside markings are macular — composed of separated lines and dots rather than solid continuous bands. The hindwing postdiscal band is widely dislocated at vein 2.
key_features: Macular underside markings (separated dots and lines, not solid bands)
HW postdiscal band widely dislocated at vein 2

g_alitaeus.description: Defined by having a central spot in FW space 11 on the underside and HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly straight or convex. Tailed species (tail 3.5–4.0 mm, except A. denta ~2.5 mm). Nine Malaysian species.
key_features: Central spot in FW underside space 11
HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly straight or convex
Tailed (3.5–4.0 mm; A. denta only ~2.5 mm)
Male aedeagus right-angled before apex (dissection required)

g_agrata_ace.description: Recognizable by the hindwing underside central cell spot, which is band-like and stretches entirely across the cell. Two Malaysian species, separated by the FW postdiscal band dislocation and the shape of HW postdiscal spot 6.
key_features: HW underside central cell spot band-like, stretching entirely across the cell

q_agrata_ace_s1.question: Is the forewing postdiscal band clearly dislocated at vein 4, and is hindwing postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave?
q_agrata_ace_s1.hint: Arhopala ace ace has the FW band distinctly dislocated at vein 4 with HW spot 6 outwardly concave; all markings are relatively wide and the male is deep cobalt blue with a thread-like border. Arhopala azinis azinis has the FW band sinuous or only slightly dislocated at vein 4, HW spot 6 outwardly straight or convex, and the male has broad borders; it is strictly montane.

choices.label: Yes — FW band clearly dislocated at vein 4; HW spot 6 outwardly concave
No — FW band sinuous or only slightly dislocated; HW spot 6 straight or convex

r_ace_ace.name: Arhopala ace ace
r_ace_ace.note: Underside: all markings relatively wide; FW postdiscal band dislocated at vein 4; HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave. Male: deep cobalt blue with black border reduced to a thread. Female: purple.

r_azinis_azinis.name: Arhopala azinis azinis
r_azinis_azinis.note: Underside: FW postdiscal band sinuous or only slightly dislocated at vein 4; HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly straight or convex. Male: dark blue with broad black borders (2.0 mm at FW mid-termen, 3.0 mm on HW). Female: purple. Strictly montane habitat.

g_agrata_agrata.description: Identified by specific absences on the forewing underside: the postdiscal spot in space 9 is absent or very obscure, and there is never a costal spot in space 10. One Malaysian species.
key_features: FW underside: postdiscal spot in space 9 absent or very obscure
FW underside: no costal spot in space 10
HW postdiscal spot 6 considerably wider than spot 7
All underside markings tend to be rather faint

r_agrata_agrata.name: Arhopala agrata agrata
r_agrata_agrata.note: Underside markings faint. FW: postdiscal spot in space 9 absent or very obscure; no costal spot in space 10. HW: postdiscal spot 6 considerably wider than spot 7. Male: dark purplish blue with black border reduced to a thread. Female: bright blue to purple-blue; HW blue reaches the termen between vein endings.

g_cleander.description: Relatively large butterflies with a conspicuously projecting tornal lobe on the hindwing. The postdiscal band is dislocated at vein 2, with spots 6 and 7 placed vertically.
key_features: Relatively large species
Conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe
Postdiscal band dislocated at vein 2; spots 6 and 7 placed vertically

g_cleander_alea.description: Smaller species within the cleander group with a less conspicuous tornal lobe.
key_features: Smaller than typical cleander
Less conspicuous HW tornal lobe
Postdiscal band dislocated at vein 2

g_eumolphus.description: Tailed species where the forewing postdiscal spot 4 is moved out of line with the others. On the hindwing, postdiscal spot 6 is placed directly below spot 7. This group includes species with shining green males.
key_features: Tailed
FW postdiscal spot 4 out of line with the other spots
HW postdiscal spot 6 placed directly below spot 7
Some males: shining green upperside

g_vihara.description: Tailed species where the forewing postdiscal spot 4 is in continuation with spots 5 and 6. The hindwing postdiscal spot 6 is rounded and more or less separated from adjacent spots.
key_features: Tailed
FW postdiscal spot 4 in continuation with spots 5 and 6
HW postdiscal spot 6 rounded and more or less separated

g_rama.description: Hindwing postdiscal spot 6 is outwardly sinuous (wavy outer edge). There is almost always a costal spot in FW space 10 on the underside.
key_features: HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly sinuous (wavy outer edge)
Costal spot almost always present in FW space 10

g_agelastus.description: FW underside postdiscal band is continuous; often a spot at base of FW space 3. Most species lack a costal spot in FW space 10 (exception: epimete suedas). HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Most species are tailless; males typically have broad black borders. Seven Malaysian species in four subgroups.
key_features: FW postdiscal band continuous; often spot at base of FW space 3
HW postdiscal band partially dislocated at vein 2
Most species tailless (barami penanga is the exception)
Males: broad black borders, often similar to females

g_corinda.description: Features a long filamentous tail approximately 3.5 mm in length. On the forewing underside, the basal half of space 1b is completely darkened.
key_features: Very long filamentous tail (~3.5 mm)
FW underside: basal half of space 1b completely darkened

g_aurea.description: Males have a hindwing cell considerably longer than half the wing length. Male forewings are acute with a straight or concave termen. Males are shining green.
key_features: Male: HW cell considerably longer than half wing length
Male: FW apex acute; termen straight or concave
Male upperside: shining green
Tailed

g_ganesa.description: Small species with spots above the cell on the forewing underside and a spot at the base of FW space 3. All have short tails.
key_features: Small species
Spots above the FW cell on the underside
Spot at base of FW space 3
Short tails (all species)

g_fulla.description: Tailless species lacking an end-cell bar on the forewing underside. Underside markings are usually obscure or very faint.
key_features: Tailless
FW underside: no end-cell bar
Underside markings usually obscure

g_abseus.description: Features a short white-tipped tail at vein 3 of the hindwing (not the usual vein 2), and a spot at the base of hindwing space 1a. Forewing vein 9 is very short, arising well beyond the end of vein 10. Two species in Malaysia.
key_features: Short white-tipped tail at HW vein 3
Spot at base of HW space 1a
FW vein 9 very short (arises well beyond end of vein 10)

q_abseus_species.question: What is the underside ground colour and are the markings darkened?
q_abseus_species.hint: The key distinction is the intensity of the underside markings and the presence or absence of a costal spot in forewing space 12.

choices.label: Markings much darkened (blackish brown); costal spot present in FW space 12
Ground colour hair brown; markings not prominently darkened; no spot in FW space 12

r_abseus_abseus.name: Arhopala abseus
r_abseus_abseus.note: Underside markings much darkened — most spots blackish brown. Forewing underside: costal spot in space 12; two spots each in spaces 9, 10, and 11. Hindwing: spot in middle of space 7 outlined in whitish. Male upperside: deep lustrous violet blue; FW black border expands from 2.0 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at apex. Female: shining blue with wider borders.

r_anella.name: Arhopala anella
r_anella.note: Underside ground colour hair brown; markings not prominently darkened. Forewing underside lacks a spot in space 12. Male upperside: blue restricted to basal halves of FW spaces 1b, 2, and 3 and the lower half of the cell; HW blue confined almost entirely to the cell. Female: relatively narrow black borders.

g_aedias.description: Characterised by a very long filamentous tail at vein 2 (~6.0 mm). HW postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 are in echelon. No dark area under the FW cell, and no spot at the base of HW space 6. One Malaysian species.
key_features: Long filamentous tail at vein 2 (~6.0 mm)
HW postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 in echelon
No dark area under the FW cell
No spot at base of HW space 6

r_aedias.name: Arhopala aedias
r_aedias.note: Underside ground colour hair brown; spots narrowly outlined in pale buff lines. Male upperside: lustrous violet blue with black border reduced to a thread. Female upperside: purple blue with typical broad black borders. Two size forms occur: large (FW 25–29 mm) and small (FW 20–22 mm; small form has more rounded, darker spots beneath). Two subspecies: A. aedias agnis (forest at low to moderate elevations, standard) and A. aedias meritatas (Langkawi Islands — paler and brighter blue upperside in both sexes).

q_agelastus_s1.question: Is there a costal spot in forewing space 10?
q_agelastus_s1.hint: A costal spot in FW space 10 is unique to Arhopala epimete suedas within this group. All other agelastus group species lack it.

choices.label: Yes — costal spot present in FW space 10
No — FW space 10 lacks a costal spot

q_agelastus_s2.question: Does the hindwing have a tail?
q_agelastus_s2.hint: Within the agelastus group, only Arhopala barami penanga is tailed. All other species are tailless.

choices.label: Yes — hindwing is tailed
No — tailless

q_agelastus_s3.question: Are the underside markings small and macular (dot-like), and are tornal green scales absent from the hindwing?
q_agelastus_s3.hint: Arhopala cardoni has characteristically small, macular (isolated dot-like) markings on the underside. The hindwing also lacks the metallic tornal green scales seen in some other species. The HW is evenly rounded.

choices.label: Yes — small macular markings; no tornal green scales; HW evenly rounded
No — markings larger or not macular

q_agelastus_s4.question: Are the inner submarginal spots in FW spaces 2–3 and HW spaces 4–5 notably enlarged and darkened, and is HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave or sinuous?
q_agelastus_s4.hint: Arhopala alaconia media (alesia subgroup) is distinguished by strikingly enlarged and darkened inner submarginal spots in these specific spaces, combined with an outwardly concave or sinuous HW postdiscal spot 6.

choices.label: Yes — inner submarginal spots enlarged and darkened; HW spot 6 outwardly concave or sinuous
No

q_agelastus_s5.question: Is the male upperside notably darker violet with narrower black borders than typical for the group?
q_agelastus_s5.hint: Arhopala labuana has a distinctly darker, deeper violet upperside and narrower black borders compared to A. agelastus and A. wildeyana, which are both violet blue.

choices.label: Yes — much darker violet; borders notably narrower
No — violet blue

q_agelastus_s6.question: How broad is the male forewing black border?
q_agelastus_s6.hint: Wildeyana has broader borders (2.0 mm at tornus to 5.0 mm at costa) than agelastus (1.0 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at apex). Females: wildeyana is brighter/purer blue; agelastus female is brighter blue with broader borders; in agelastus, the underside HW space 3 spot is nearly in line with spots 2 and 4.

choices.label: Broad: 2.0 mm at tornus to 5.0 mm at costa
Standard: 1.0 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at apex

r_epimete_suedas.name: Arhopala epimete suedas
r_epimete_suedas.note: Unique in the agelastus group: costal spot present in FW space 10. FW underside: lower portion of postdiscal band significantly wider than upper part. HW lacks tornal green scales. Both sexes alike: blue with broad FW borders (2.0 mm at tornus to 6.0 mm at costa). Blue on HW confined to the cell. Tailless.

r_barami_penanga.name: Arhopala barami penanga
r_barami_penanga.note: The only tailed species in the agelastus group. Male upperside: blue with a broad FW border expanding from 2.5 mm at the tornus to 8.0 mm at the costa. Female: paler blue.

r_cardoni.name: Arhopala cardoni
r_cardoni.note: Monotypic subgroup. Underside markings small and macular (dot-like). HW tailless and evenly rounded; no tornal green scales. Male: bright blue with violet tinge; FW border 2.0 mm at tornus expanding to 5.0 mm at apex. Female: purer blue with slightly wider black borders than the male.

r_alaconia_media.name: Arhopala alaconia media
r_alaconia_media.note: Alesia subgroup. Underside: inner submarginal spots in FW spaces 2–3 and HW spaces 4–5 notably enlarged and darkened; HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave or sinuous. Normally tailless (slightly toothed), but a rare tailed form (f. kempi) exists. Both sexes alike: violet blue with FW border 3.0 mm at tornus expanding to mid-costa.

r_labuana.name: Arhopala labuana
r_labuana.note: Male upperside: much darker violet than A. agelastus, with narrower black borders. Female: dark blue. Tailless.

r_wildeyana.name: Arhopala wildeyana wildeyana
r_wildeyana.note: Male upperside: violet blue with broader borders than A. labuana or A. agelastus — 2.0 mm at tornus to 5.0 mm at costa. Female: brighter and purer blue. Tailless.

r_agelastus_agelastus.name: Arhopala agelastus agelastus
r_agelastus_agelastus.note: Underside: HW postdiscal spot in space 3 is almost in line with spots in spaces 2 and 4 (distinguishes female from allied species). Male: violet blue with FW border 1.0 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at apex. Female: brighter blue with broader borders. Tailless.

q_agesias_s1.question: How many spots are in forewing space 11, and what is the underside ground colour?
q_agesias_s1.hint: Arhopala kinabala is distinguished by having TWO spots in FW space 11, a dark brown ground colour, and blackish-brown markings. A. agesias and A. similis have a single spot in space 11 and a hair-brown ground colour.

choices.label: Two spots in FW space 11; ground colour dark brown; markings blackish-brown
Single spot in FW space 11; ground colour hair brown

q_agesias_s2.question: Is the postdiscal band completely absent from the forewing underside?
q_agesias_s2.hint: Arhopala similis is superficially identical to A. agesias but uniquely lacks the postdiscal band entirely on the FW underside. A. agesias has the normal rounded-spot postdiscal band present.

choices.label: Yes — postdiscal band completely absent from FW underside
No — postdiscal band present (rounded spots)

r_kinabala.name: Arhopala kinabala
r_kinabala.note: Underside ground colour dark brown with large, blackish-brown markings. FW space 11 has two spots; FW space 3 spot is elongate and touches the end-cell bar. HW postdiscal spot 6 is oval, significantly enlarged, and overlaps the end-cell bar. Rare; seldom found below 450 m elevation.

r_similis.name: Arhopala similis
r_similis.note: Superficially identical to A. agesias but uniquely lacks the postdiscal band on the FW underside. Status as a separate species (vs. dimorph of A. agesias) has been questioned, though female genitalia differ slightly.

r_agesias.name: Arhopala agesias
r_agesias.note: The most common species in the group. Underside: hair-brown ground colour with all markings as rounded spots; single central spot in FW space 11.

q_alitaeus_s1.question: Is there a spot at the extreme base of forewing underside space 10 (just above vein 10, near the cell-end)?
q_alitaeus_s1.hint: Look for a small isolated spot right at the base of FW space 10, distinct from the regular postdiscal row. Species with this spot: pseudomuta, sintanga, alitaeus, denta. Species without: elopura, arianaga, ariana, aida, havilandi.

choices.label: Yes — spot present at extreme base of FW space 10
No — no spot at extreme base of FW space 10

q_alitaeus_s1_tail.question: Is the hindwing tail notably short, approximately 2.5 mm (rather than the typical 3.5–4.0 mm for the group)?
q_alitaeus_s1_tail.hint: A. denta is the only alitaeus-group member with a distinctly shortened tail (~2.5 mm). All others have tails of 3.5–4.0 mm.

choices.label: Yes — tail short, ~2.5 mm
No — tail of normal length, 3.5–4.0 mm

q_alitaeus_s1_color.question: Is the underside pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings?
q_alitaeus_s1_color.hint: A. sintanga has a distinctly pale slatey-purple ground colour with markings that strongly contrast in reddish-brown. A. pseudomuta and A. alitaeus are hair-brown underneath.

choices.label: Yes — pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings
No — hair-brown ground colour

q_alitaeus_s1_marks.question: Are the underside markings notably annular (ring-like) and is the FW postdiscal spot in space 1b often dislocated outward relative to the spot in space 2?
q_alitaeus_s1_marks.hint: A. alitaeus has more annular (ring-like) markings than allied species, and the space 1b spot is often shifted outward; male has regular black borders at least 1.0 mm wide. A. pseudomuta has markings that are hardly darker than the hair-brown ground colour; HW space 6 is slightly oblique and does not overlap the end-cell bar.

choices.label: Yes — annular markings; FW space 1b spot often dislocated outward; borders ≥1.0 mm
No — markings barely darker than ground colour; HW space 6 slightly oblique

q_alitaeus_s2_color.question: Is the underside pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings?
q_alitaeus_s2_color.hint: A. havilandi has a pale slatey-purple ground colour with strongly contrasting reddish-brown markings and a thread-like black border on the male upperside. If the underside is hair-brown or dark brownish, choose No.

choices.label: Yes — pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings
No — hair-brown or dark brownish underside

q_alitaeus_s2_glaze.question: Is the underside dark brownish (not hair-brown) with a distinct slatey or purplish glaze?
q_alitaeus_s2_glaze.hint: A. ariana is dark brown with a faint purplish wash; A. aida is brown with a slatey to purplish glaze and a slightly produced FW apex. A. elopura and A. arianaga are hair-brown with markings barely darker than the ground colour.

choices.label: Yes — dark brownish with a slatey or purplish glaze
No — hair-brown, markings barely darker than ground colour

q_alitaeus_s2_term.question: Is the forewing termen notably straight and the apex slightly produced (pointed)?
q_alitaeus_s2_term.hint: A. aida has a distinctly straight FW termen with a slightly produced apex, and the male black border expands from ~1.25 mm at the tornus to ~2.0 mm at the apex. A. ariana has a more normal wing shape; FW postdiscal spot 4 is shifted distad out of line with adjacent spots.

choices.label: Yes — FW termen straight; apex slightly produced; border widens toward apex
No — FW shape normal; postdiscal spot 4 shifted distad; border 1.0–1.5 mm

q_alitaeus_s2_sp6.question: Is the hindwing postdiscal space 6 partly fuscous (darkened)?
q_alitaeus_s2_sp6.hint: A. elopura has HW space 6 partly fuscous (dark). In A. arianaga, HW space 6 is entirely blue; underside markings are slightly darker than the ground colour with a faint pinkish glaze, and the male border is reduced to a thread.

choices.label: Yes — HW space 6 partly fuscous; male upperside purer blue, border ~0.5 mm
No — HW space 6 entirely blue; faint pinkish glaze; male border thread-like

r_pseudomuta.name: Arhopala pseudomuta
r_pseudomuta.note: Underside hair-brown; markings hardly darker than ground colour. FW: basal spot present in space 10. HW: postdiscal space 6 slightly oblique, does not overlap end-cell bar. Male: violet-blue with black border ~1.0 mm. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_elopura.name: Arhopala elopura
r_elopura.note: Underside hair-brown; markings barely darker than ground colour — contrast less than in A. pseudomuta. FW: no basal spot in space 10. HW: postdiscal space 6 partly fuscous. Male: purer blue with black border ~0.5 mm. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_arianaga.name: Arhopala arianaga
r_arianaga.note: Underside markings slightly darker than ground colour with a very faint pinkish glaze. FW: no basal spot in space 10. HW: postdiscal space 6 entirely blue. Male: black border reduced to a thread. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_ariana.name: Arhopala ariana
r_ariana.note: Underside dark brown with a faint purplish wash. FW: no basal spot in space 10; postdiscal spot in space 4 shifted distad out of line. Male: border 1.0–1.5 mm, slightly wider at apex. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_aida.name: Arhopala aida
r_aida.note: Underside brown with a slatey to purplish glaze. FW: no basal spot in space 10; termen straight, apex slightly produced. Male: border expands from ~1.25 mm at tornus to ~2.0 mm at apex. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_havilandi.name: Arhopala havilandi
r_havilandi.note: Underside pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings. FW: no basal spot in space 10. Male: violet-blue, black border reduced to a thread. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_sintanga.name: Arhopala sintanga
r_sintanga.note: Underside pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings. FW: basal spot present in space 10. Male: dark violet, black border reduced to a thread. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_alitaeus.name: Arhopala alitaeus
r_alitaeus.note: Underside markings notably annular (ring-like); FW postdiscal spot in space 1b often dislocated outward from spot in space 2. FW: basal spot present in space 10. Male: regular black borders ≥1.0 mm wide. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_denta.name: Arhopala denta
r_denta.note: Distinctive SHORT tail — only ~2.5 mm (others in the group have 3.5–4.0 mm). FW: basal spot present in space 10. Underside hair-brown with at most a slight pinkish glaze. Male: brilliant blue, black border reduced to a thread.

r_kurzi.name: Arhopala kurzi
r_kurzi.note: Tailless with a distinctly rounded HW tornus. HW postdiscal band widely dislocated at vein 2. Male: purple-blue with a thread-like black border. Single member of the kurzi subgroup.

q_amph_s1.question: Does the hindwing have a short tail (tooth-like projection) at vein 2?
q_amph_s1.hint: A. delta is the ONLY tailed member of the amphimuta subgroup. All other amphimuta subgroup species are tailless.

choices.label: Yes — tailed at vein 2
No — tailless

q_amph_s2.question: On the forewing underside, is the basal cell spot absent?
q_amph_s2.hint: A. avathina avathina is recognized by the absence of the basal cell spot on the FW underside; additionally, the HW postdiscal band is completely dislocated at vein 2. All other amphimuta subgroup members have a basal cell spot.

choices.label: Yes — no basal cell spot on FW underside (HW band also completely dislocated at vein 2)
No — basal cell spot present on FW underside

q_amph_s3.question: Is there a distinct white patch on the hindwing cilia just above the tornal lobe?
q_amph_s3.hint: A. norda has a small but distinctive white patch on the HW cilia just above the tornal lobe, and also has less rounded (more angular) postdiscal spots compared to A. major.

choices.label: Yes — white patch on HW cilia above tornal lobe; postdiscal spots less rounded
No — no white patch on HW cilia

q_amph_s4.question: Is the male's black border broad, 1.5 mm or wider?
q_amph_s4.hint: A. major has a border expanding from 2.0 mm at tornus to 3.0 mm at apex (blue upperside). A. stubbsi has a broad ~2.0 mm border but is dull bluish purple. All others have narrow borders (0.5 mm or thread-like).

choices.label: Yes — border ≥1.5 mm wide
No — border narrow (0.5 mm or thread-like)

q_amph_broad.question: Is the male distinctly blue above, with the border widening from ~2.0 mm at the tornus to ~3.0 mm at the apex?
q_amph_broad.hint: A. major major is bluer than A. amphimuta; its border expands toward the apex. A. stubbsi is dull bluish purple with a broad but even ~2.0 mm border.

choices.label: Yes — distinctly blue; border expands 2.0→3.0 mm from tornus to apex
No — dull bluish purple; border broad but even (~2.0 mm)

q_amph_s5.question: Is the male's black border reduced to a thread (very narrow, well under 0.5 mm)?
q_amph_s5.hint: Thread-like border species: A. moolaiana yajuna (shining violet-blue; female blue reaches HW termen) and A. alica (dark dull purple; Bornean endemic). Regular 0.5 mm border species: A. amphimuta, A. sceva, A. zylda, A. baluensis, A. dajagaka.

choices.label: Yes — border thread-like
No — border regular ~0.5 mm

q_amph_thread.question: Is the male upperside a shining violet-blue, and does the female's blue colour extend to the hindwing termen?
q_amph_thread.hint: A. moolaiana yajuna: shining violet-blue male; female blue reaches HW termen. A. alica: dark, dull purple male; Bornean endemic — found only in Borneo, not the Malay Peninsula.

choices.label: Yes — shining violet-blue; female blue reaches HW termen (Peninsula)
No — dark dull purple; female blue does not reach HW termen (Bornean)

q_amph_s6.question: Is the hindwing underside postdiscal series dislocated at vein 2, and is the species noticeably smaller than its allies?
q_amph_s6.hint: A. sceva indra has the HW postdiscal series clearly dislocated at vein 2 and is a smaller species; male is dark violet-blue. In the remaining species (amphimuta, zylda, baluensis, dajagaka), the HW postdiscal band is not strongly dislocated at vein 2.

choices.label: Yes — HW postdiscal series dislocated at vein 2; smaller species; male dark violet-blue
No — HW band not strongly dislocated at vein 2

q_amph_s7.question: Is the male a warm violet-purple (with a reddish hue rather than blue), and is the FW postdiscal spot in space 3 an oblique oval?
q_amph_s7.hint: A. amphimuta amphimuta is violet-purple (warmer/redder than violet-blue) with the FW spot 3 usually an oblique oval, and the FW band slightly dislocated at vein 4. A. zylda, A. baluensis, and A. dajagaka are more violet-blue.

choices.label: Yes — warm violet-purple; FW spot 3 oblique oval; FW band slightly dislocated at vein 4
No — violet-blue (not warm purple)

q_amph_s8.question: Does the female have exceptionally narrow borders (narrower than typical 0.5 mm)?
q_amph_s8.hint: A. zylda elioti: female has exceptionally narrow borders (a diagnostic feature). A. baluensis and A. dajagaka are Bornean endemics. If the specimen is from the Malay Peninsula, it is most likely A. zylda.

choices.label: Yes — female exceptionally narrow-bordered (Peninsula species)
No — female with normal borders (Bornean endemic)

q_amph_borneo.question: Does the female have unusually thick black borders and distinctly darker venation on the upperside?
q_amph_borneo.hint: A. dajagaka: female has thick black borders and darker venation. A. baluensis: HW cilia lacks the white spot in space 1a that is present in some allied species.

choices.label: Yes — female with thick black borders and darker venation
No — HW cilia lacks white spot in space 1a; female without thick borders

r_delta.name: Arhopala delta
r_delta.note: The ONLY tailed member of the amphimuta subgroup — short tooth-like tail at vein 2. Male: deep violet with a narrow, regular black border. Part of the amphimuta subgroup (HW spots 5–7 in echelon; spots rounded).

r_avathina_avathina.name: Arhopala avathina avathina
r_avathina_avathina.note: Distinctive: FW underside lacks a basal cell spot (absent, not obscure). HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2. Otherwise similar to other amphimuta subgroup members.

r_norda.name: Arhopala norda
r_norda.note: Distinguished from A. major by the small white patch on the HW cilia just above the tornal lobe, and by less rounded (more angular) postdiscal spots on the underside.

r_major_major.name: Arhopala major major
r_major_major.note: Both sexes bluer than A. amphimuta above. Male: FW black border expands from 2.0 mm at tornus to 3.0 mm at apex. Underside similar to A. amphimuta. HW cilia often prolonged into a short tooth at vein 2.

r_stubbsi.name: Arhopala stubbsi
r_stubbsi.note: Male: rather dull bluish-purple with a broad, even ~2.0 mm black border — the widest border in the amphimuta subgroup. HW cilia often prolonged at vein 2.

r_moolaiana_yajuna.name: Arhopala moolaiana yajuna
r_moolaiana_yajuna.note: Male: shining violet-blue with a thread-like black border. Female: blue colour extends to the HW termen (diagnostic). HW cilia often prolonged into a short tooth at vein 2.

r_alica.name: Arhopala alica
r_alica.note: Bornean endemic. Male: dark, dull purple with a thread-like black border. Distinguished from A. moolaiana by its darker, duller coloration and Bornean range.

r_sceva_indra.name: Arhopala sceva indra
r_sceva_indra.note: A smaller species. HW underside postdiscal series is dislocated at vein 2 (distinctive within the amphimuta subgroup). Male: dark violet-blue.

r_amphimuta_amphimuta.name: Arhopala amphimuta amphimuta
r_amphimuta_amphimuta.note: Male: violet-purple (warmer/redder hue than violet-blue) with a regular ~0.5 mm black border. FW underside: postdiscal band slightly dislocated at vein 4; spot in space 3 usually an oblique oval. HW cilia often prolonged into a short tooth at vein 2.

r_zylda_elioti.name: Arhopala zylda elioti
r_zylda_elioti.note: Male: violet-blue with a regular ~0.5 mm black border. Female: exceptionally narrow borders (diagnostic — narrower than in allied species). Malay Peninsula.

r_dajagaka.name: Arhopala dajagaka
r_dajagaka.note: Bornean endemic. Male: dark bluish-purple with a ~0.5 mm border. Female: distinctively thick black borders and darker venation on the upperside.

r_baluensis.name: Arhopala baluensis
r_baluensis.note: Bornean endemic. HW cilia lacks the white spot in space 1a (present in some allied species). Male: dark purple-blue with a ~0.5 mm border.

q_muta_s1.question: Is the species very small and duller bluish-purple on the male upperside?
q_muta_s1.hint: A. tropaea is noticeably smaller than A. muta and A. moorei, with a duller bluish-purple male upperside. A. muta and A. moorei are larger and brighter blue.

choices.label: Yes — very small; male duller bluish-purple
No — larger; male bright blue

q_muta_s2.question: On the male upperside, are both the forewing and hindwing the same shade of blue?
q_muta_s2.hint: A. moorei busa: both wings the same shade of blue in the male. A. muta maranda: FW is bright blue but the HW is a slightly paler, more glistening blue.

choices.label: Yes — both wings same shade of blue in male
No — FW brighter blue; HW slightly paler, more glistening

r_tropaea.name: Arhopala tropaea
r_tropaea.note: Very small species. Male: duller bluish-purple upperside. Underside: postdiscal spots quadrate/banded; FW space 2 spot stepped toward termen (muta subgroup).

r_moorei_busa.name: Arhopala moorei busa
r_moorei_busa.note: Similar to A. muta maranda. Male: both wings the same shade of blue on the upperside (in A. muta the FW is brighter than the HW). Underside: spots quadrate; FW space 2 spot stepped toward termen.

r_muta_maranda.name: Arhopala muta maranda
r_muta_maranda.note: Male: bright blue FW; HW slightly paler, more glistening blue. Underside: postdiscal spots quadrate/banded; FW postdiscal spot in space 2 stepped outward toward the termen.

q_perimuta_s1.question: Is there a yellowish discal area on the hindwing underside with obscure markings and no tornal green scales?
q_perimuta_s1.hint: A. perimuta regina is easily recognized by the yellowish (not white or grey) discal area on the HW underside, with markings that are obscure or almost absent, and the complete absence of the tornal green scales found in many Arhopala. A. metamuta metamuta has a striking contrast — brilliant greenish-blue HW vs. dark violet-blue FW on the male upperside.

choices.label: Yes — yellowish HW discal area; markings obscure; no tornal green scales
No — HW underside normal; male HW brilliant greenish-blue contrasting with dark violet-blue FW

r_perimuta_regina.name: Arhopala perimuta regina
r_perimuta_regina.note: Easily recognized: HW underside has a yellowish discal area with obscure or almost absent markings; tornal green scales completely absent. FW postdiscal spots 2 and 3 are in line (perimuta subgroup).

r_metamuta_metamuta.name: Arhopala metamuta metamuta
r_metamuta_metamuta.note: Striking upperside: male has a brilliant greenish-blue HW that contrasts strongly with the dark, shining violet-blue FW. FW postdiscal spots 2 and 3 are in line (perimuta subgroup).

g_needs_more_info.description: The specimen does not match any of the features checked so far, or requires features not yet covered by this key. Some closely related species (e.g. in the mineus or alea subgroups) require male genitalia dissection for certain identification.
key_features: Review all previous steps for any missed features
Consider genitalia dissection for difficult cases
Refer to specialised literature
